title: Ensure that the --insecure-port argument is set to 0

input_row.title: Ensure that the --insecure-port argument is set to 0
input_row.description: Do not bind to insecure port.
input_row.rationale: Setting up the federation apiserver to serve on an insecure port would allow unauthenticated and unencrypted access to it. It is assumed that firewall rules are set up such that this port is not reachable from outside of the cluster. But, as a defense in depth measure, you should not use an insecure port.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-apiserver Verify that the --insecure-port argument is set to 0 .
input_row.remediation: Edit the deployment specs and set --insecure-port=0 . kubectl edit deployments federation-apiserver-deployment --namespace=federation-system Impact: None
input_row.default_value: By default, the insecure port is set to 8080.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_insecure_port_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the command-line arguments of the federation API server.
- The specific API call to use is `kubectl get deployment federation-apiserver-deployment -n federation-system -o json`.
- The command will retrieve the deployment configuration for the federation API server, including the command-line arguments.
- Validate the `args` field in the deployment spec to ensure that the `--insecure-port` argument is set to `0`.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: The `--insecure-port` argument is present and set to `0`.
  - Fail: The `--insecure-port` argument is either not present or set to any value other than `0`.
